summary: DHH highlights Matt Haughey (MetaFilter creator) discovering Instiki as a powerful personal notebook replacement. Matt switched from a plain text todo file to Instiki and found it transformed his personal organization through hyperlinked wiki pages. DHH uses this as a promotional moment for Instiki, noting its growing adoption with over three thousand downloads, four hundred of which were for the Mac OS X version.
key_points: Wiki software can serve as a superior personal notebook compared to plain text files
Instiki's instant-on OS X version was driving significant adoption among Mac users
Endorsement from a respected web creator (Matt Haughey) validated Instiki's usefulness
money_quotes: As a private scratchpad, it's absolutely fantastic.
Over the past few days I've built out my ToDos into a pile of organized, hyperlinked pages that are useful to have around.
Are you collecting your thoughts in Instiki yet?
key_insight: Wiki software like Instiki can replace simple text files as a personal notebook by adding the power of hyperlinked, organized pages.
